source_lessons.title: Market default should come from preference context
Include direct and indirect competitors across the industry track
Make PRD analysis outputs visually reviewable

skill_updates.lesson_title: Market default should come from preference context
skill_updates.principle: If the user does not specify a market, resolve market scope from the current request, active project cache, or approved user preference; generic skills must not hard-code a market.
skill_updates.expected_behavior: Source briefs should include market_default only when it was supplied by the request or approved preference context.
skill_updates.verification_hint: A new PRD analysis run without explicit market should either use an approved preference-backed market or mark market as unresolved instead of silently assuming a region.

skill_updates.lesson_title: Market default should come from preference context
skill_updates.principle: Competitor analysis should read market scope from the request or approved preference context instead of embedding a fixed default market in the skill.
skill_updates.expected_behavior: Research should prioritize sources and competitor categories appropriate to the resolved market; if market is unresolved, ask or mark the assumption.
skill_updates.verification_hint: Competitor output should state the source of its market assumption: explicit request, project cache, user preference, or unresolved assumption.

skill_updates.lesson_title: Include direct and indirect competitors across the industry track
skill_updates.principle: Competitor scope includes direct tools, adjacent platforms, platform-native substitutes, content/community substitutes, offline/service substitutes, and manual workarounds when they influence the user's decision, attention, time, or workflow.
skill_updates.expected_behavior: Competitor output should explain the industry-track influence map instead of only listing vertical products.
skill_updates.verification_hint: A competitor artifact should include at least direct, adjacent, platform-native, and workaround categories unless a category is explicitly irrelevant.

skill_updates.lesson_title: Include direct and indirect competitors across the industry track
skill_updates.principle: Scenario ranking should use competitor influence categories, not only direct vertical competitors, when scoring differentiation and risk.
skill_updates.expected_behavior: Differentiation and risk scores should consider content platforms, platform-native substitutes, and manual workarounds when they affect the scenario.
skill_updates.verification_hint: Scenario ranking should link each core scenario to the relevant competitor influence category.

skill_updates.lesson_title: Include direct and indirect competitors across the industry track
skill_updates.principle: MVP scoping should account for direct and indirect competitor influence before deciding what belongs in MVP, V1, Later, or Non-goal.
skill_updates.expected_behavior: MVP scope should avoid both overbuilding against heavy platforms and underestimating platform-native or manual substitutes.
skill_updates.verification_hint: MVP scope should reference competitor influence when explaining included and deferred items.

skill_updates.lesson_title: Make PRD analysis outputs visually reviewable
skill_updates.principle: When user groups are broad, include a review matrix before narrowing so the user can quickly inspect priorities.
skill_updates.expected_behavior: User-universe artifacts should include a matrix or equivalent first-screen visualization for broad segmentation work.
skill_updates.verification_hint: The summary or artifact should show segments by priority/fit and pain/frequency before the narrowed recommendation.

skill_updates.lesson_title: Make PRD analysis outputs visually reviewable
skill_updates.principle: When a workflow involves multiple skills or MCPs, make the evidence and responsibility path visually reviewable.
skill_updates.expected_behavior: Source collection should preserve enough trace data for downstream summaries to draw skill/MCP role boundaries.
skill_updates.verification_hint: Workflow summaries should show which skill/MCP collected, analyzed, ranked, scoped, and audited.

skill_updates.lesson_title: Make PRD analysis outputs visually reviewable
skill_updates.principle: Competitor outputs should be reviewable through category maps and role-boundary diagrams when the competitor set spans multiple categories.
skill_updates.expected_behavior: Competitor summaries should show direct, adjacent, platform-native, content/community, and workaround categories in an easy-to-scan structure.
skill_updates.verification_hint: The artifact should not force the user to read a long competitor list before understanding the market map.

approval_checklist: User confirms where market defaults should live: user preference, project cache, or current request only.
User confirms competitor scope should include direct and indirect influence categories across the industry track.
User confirms matrix and flowchart outputs should become default behavior for broad PRD analysis work.
After approval, update only the affected plugin Skill files and output contracts, then run harness and regression.
